basicPropertyValues.val: Application ontology for automotive circular-economy product, material, sustainability, usage, dismantling, engineering and transactional data aligned to PMDCore and BFO 2020.
https://w3id.org/pmd/automatce.owl#CC-BY-4.0
Automotive Circular Economy Ontology
(Elucidation) An entity is anything that exists or has existed or will exist
Julius Caesar; the Second World War; your body mass index; Verdi's Requiem
(Elucidation) A continuant is an entity that persists, endures, or continues to exist through time while maintaining its identity
A human being; a tennis ball; a cave; a region of space; someone's temperature
(Elucidation) An occurrent is an entity that unfolds itself in time or it is the start or end of such an entity or it is a temporal or spatiotemporal region
As for process, history, process boundary, spatiotemporal region, zero-dimensional temporal region, one-dimensional temporal region, temporal interval, temporal instant.
b is an independent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is no c such that b specifically depends on c or b generically depends on c
An atom; a molecule; an organism; a heart; a chair; the bottom right portion of a human torso; a leg; the interior of your mouth; a spatial region; an orchestra
(Elucidation) p is a process means p is an occurrent that has some temporal proper part and for some time t, p has some material entity as participant
An act of selling; the life of an organism; a process of sleeping; a process of cell-division; a beating of the heart; a process of meiosis; the taxiing of an aircraft; the programming of a computer

(Elucidation) A disposition b is a realizable entity such that if b ceases to exist then its bearer is physically changed & b's realization occurs when and because this bearer is in some special physical circumstances & this realization occurs in virtue of the bearer's physical make-up

An atom of element X has the disposition to decay to an atom of element Y; the cell wall is disposed to transport cellular material through endocytosis and exocytosis; certain people have a predisposition to colon cancer; children are innately disposed to categorize objects in certain ways

(Elucidation) A realizable entity is a specifically dependent continuant that inheres in some independent continuant which is not a spatial region & which is of a type some instances of which are realized in processes of a correlated type

The role of being a doctor; the role of this boundary to delineate where Utah and Colorado meet; the function of your reproductive organs; the disposition of your blood to coagulate; the disposition of this piece of metal to conduct electricity
(Elucidation) A quality is a specifically dependent continuant that, in contrast to roles and dispositions, does not require any further process in order to be realized

The colour of a tomato; the ambient temperature of this portion of air; the length of the circumference of your waist; the shape of your nose; the shape of your nostril; the mass of this piece of gold
b is a specifically dependent continuant =Def b is a continuant & there is some independent continuant c which is not a spatial region & which is such that b specifically depends on c
(with multiple bearers) John's love for Mary; the ownership relation between John and this statue; the relation of authority between John and his subordinates

(with one bearer) The mass of this tomato; the pink colour of a medium rare piece of grilled filet mignon at its centre; the smell of this portion of mozzarella; the disposition of this fish to decay; the role of being a doctor; the function of this heart to pump blood; the shape of this hole

(Elucidation) A role b is a realizable entity such that b exists because there is some single bearer that is in some special physical, social, or institutional set of circumstances in which this bearer does not have to be & b is not such that, if it ceases to exist, then the physical make-up of the bearer is thereby changed

The priest role; the student role; the role of subject in a clinical trial; the role of a stone in marking a property boundary; the role of a boundary to demarcate two neighbouring administrative territories; the role of a building in serving as a military target
(Elucidation) A fiat object part b is a material entity & such that if b exists then it is continuant part of some object c & demarcated from the remainder of c by one or more fiat surfaces
The upper and lower lobes of the left lung; the dorsal and ventral surfaces of the body; the Western hemisphere of the Earth; the FMA:regional parts of an intact human body
(Elucidation) An object aggregate is a material entity consisting exactly of a plurality (≥1) of objects as member parts which together form a unit

The aggregate of the musicians in a symphony orchestra and their instruments; the aggregate of bearings in a constant velocity axle joint; the nitrogen atoms in the atmosphere; a collection of cells in a blood biobank

(Elucidation) A site is a three-dimensional immaterial entity whose boundaries either (partially or wholly) coincide with the boundaries of one or more material entities or have locations determined in relation to some material entity

A hole in a portion of cheese; a rabbit hole; the Grand Canyon; the Piazza San Marco; the kangaroo-joey-containing hole of a kangaroo pouch; your left nostril (a fiat part - the opening - of your left nasal cavity); the lumen of your gut; the hold of a ship; the interior of the trunk of your car; hole in an engineered floor joist
(Elucidation) An object is a material entity which manifests causal unity & is of a type instances of which are maximal relative to the sort of causal unity manifested
An organism; a fish tank; a planet; a laptop; a valve; a block of marble; an ice cube

(Elucidation) A generically dependent continuant is an entity that exists in virtue of the fact that there is at least one of what may be multiple copies which is the content or the pattern that multiple copies would share

The pdf file on your laptop; the pdf file that is a copy thereof on my laptop; the sequence of this protein molecule; the sequence that is a copy thereof in that protein molecule; the content that is shared by a string of dots and dashes written on a page and the transmitted Morse code signal; the content of a sentence; an engineering blueprint

(Elucidation) A function is a disposition that exists in virtue of its bearer's physical make-up & this physical make-up is something the bearer possesses because it came into being either through evolution (in the case of natural biological entities) or through intentional design (in the case of artefacts) in order to realize processes of a certain sort
The function of a hammer to drive in nails; the function of a heart pacemaker to regulate the beating of a heart through electricity
(Elucidation) A material entity is an independent continuant has some portion of matter as continuant part
A human being; the undetached arm of a human being; an aggregate of human beings
b is an immaterial entity =Def b is an independent continuant which is such that there is no time t when it has a material entity as continuant part
As for fiat point, fiat line, fiat surface, site
b is a relational quality =Def b is a quality & there exists c and d such that c and d are not identical & b specifically depends on c & b specifically depends on d
A marriage bond; an instance of love; an obligation between one person and another

definition.val: A directive information entity that describes an intended process endpoint. When part of a plan specification the concretization is realized in a planned process in which the bearer tries to effect the world so that the process endpoint is achieved.
A directive information entity that describes an action the bearer will take.

Software is a plan specification composed of a series of instructions that can be 
interpreted by or directly executed by a processing unit.

An information content entity that is intended to be a truthful statement about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements.
A generically dependent continuant that is about some thing.
An information content entity whose concretizations indicate to their bearer how to realize them in a process.
The curation status of the term. The allowed values come from an enumerated list of predefined terms. See the specification of these instances for more detailed definitions of each enumerated value.
Data about an ontology part is a data item about a part of an ontology, for example a term

A directive information entity with action specifications and objective specifications as parts, and that may be concretized as a realizable entity that, if realized, is realized in a process in which the bearer tries to achieve the objectives by taking the actions specified.
A collection of information content entities intended to be understood together as a whole
A document that is the output of a publishing process.
A planned process of making information, such as literature, music, and software etc., available to the public for sale or for free.
An information content entity that is the outcome of a dubbing process and is used to refer to one instance of entity shared by a group of people to refer to that individual entity.
A planned process that provides a reference to an individual entity shared by a group of subscribers to refer to that individual entity.
A process that realizes a plan which is the concretization of a plan specification.
a role that inheres in a material entity that is realized in an assay in which data is generated about the bearer of the evaluant role
A planned process that has the objective to produce information about a material entity (the evaluant) by examining it.

An entity that can bear roles, has members, and has a set of organization rules. Members of organizations are either organizations themselves or individual people. Members can bear specific organization member roles that are determined in the organization rules. The organization rules also determine how decisions are made on behalf of the organization by the organization members.
Manufacturer role is a role which inheres in a person or organization and which is realized by a manufacturing process.
A person or organization that has a manufacturer role.
A value specification that is specifies one category out of a fixed number of nominal categories
An information content entity that specifies a value within a classification scheme or on a quantitative scale.

comments: This role is realized when the material enters the supply chain or the hopper of a processing machine.

basicPropertyValues.val: A role borne by a material entity when it functions as an input to a manufacturing process prior to transformation or processing.
Plastic granules sitting in a silo waiting to be fed into an extruder bear this role.

comments: Additives are distinct from fillers (which bulk up volume) and the matrix. They perform specific chemical or physical modifications.

basicPropertyValues.val: A role borne by a substance intentionally added in relatively small amounts to modify the processing characteristics, performance properties, or cost of a base material.
UV stabilizers added to dashboard polymers to prevent sun damage; Plasticizers added to PVC.

comments: Organic colorants (dyes) offer high color strength and brightness but may have lower thermal stability than pigments.

basicPropertyValues.val: A complex organic chemical compound used to impart color, typically soluble in the medium.
Azo dyes used in interior textile coloring.

comments: Inorganic colorants (pigments) are generally insoluble and provide opacity and high heat stability.

basicPropertyValues.val: A mineral-based chemical compound, usually an oxide or sulfide, used to impart color.
Titanium dioxide (white) used in car paints.

comments: This role can be borne by organic dyes or inorganic pigments.

basicPropertyValues.val: A role borne by an additive substance when its primary function is to impart color to a material.
Carbon black acting as a pigment to turn a tire black.

comments: For brittle materials, this is equal to tensile strength. For ductile materials, it may be lower than the yield point.

basicPropertyValues.val: The tensile stress recorded at the moment of rupture of the specimen.
A value of 150 MPa for a GF-reinforced polymer.

comments: Can be defined descriptively (Red) or metrologically (L*a*b* coordinates).

basicPropertyValues.val: The quality of an object or substance with respect to light reflected by the object, usually determined visually by measurement of hue, saturation, and brightness.
The 'Piano Black' finish on a B-pillar trim.

comments: Polyamides are semi-crystalline polymers known for good thermal and chemical resistance.

basicPropertyValues.val: A polymer containing repeating amide (-CONH-) linkages in the polymer backbone.
Nylon 6, Nylon 12, Nylon 66.
PA

comments: PA66 has a higher melting point (~260°C) than PA6 but absorbs moisture which affects dimensional stability.

basicPropertyValues.val: A semicrystalline thermoplastic polyamide synthesized from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid.
Material used for cylinder head covers and radiator end tanks.
PA66

comments: The addition of 25% glass fiber significantly increases stiffness and tensile strength compared to neat PA66.

basicPropertyValues.val: A composite material consisting of a Polyamide 66 matrix reinforced with 25% glass fiber by mass.
Structural components in the engine bay requiring high stiffness at elevated temperatures.

comments: In PMD logic, this is a disconnected aggregate because the individual filaments in a bundle are distinct objects.

basicPropertyValues.val: A class of materials that are continuous filaments or are in discrete elongated pieces, similar to lengths of thread.
Carbon fiber tow, Aramid fibers.

comments: Used extensively as a reinforcement agent in polymer matrix composites.

basicPropertyValues.val: A material consisting of numerous extremely fine fibers of glass.
E-Glass used to reinforce PA66 for automotive pedals.
GF

comments: Commonly denoted as wt%. PA66-GF25 implies a value of 0.25 or 25%.

basicPropertyValues.val: The ratio of the mass of the fiber reinforcement to the total mass of the composite material.
25% mass fraction in PA66-GF25.

comments: Volume fraction is more critical for micromechanical modeling (e.g., Rule of Mixtures) than mass fraction.

basicPropertyValues.val: The ratio of the volume of the fiber reinforcement to the total volume of the composite material.
Approximately 14-15% volume fraction corresponds to 25% mass fraction for Glass/PA66.
A unique string or code assigned by a manufacturer to identify a specific product grade.
BASF code 'A3WG6' for a specific Ultramid grade.
The brand or trade name used for marketing the material.
'Zytel' (DuPont) or 'Durethan' (Lanxess).
A string following a standard nomenclature (e.g., ISO 1043) to describe the material composition.
PA66-GF25
A globally unique identifier (GUID) or database key for the material entry.
Catena-X Material Twin ID.
Code assigned to a specific production run for traceability.
Unique identifier for a legal entity in the automotive supply chain network (Catena-X standard).
BPNL00000003AY2 (Example BPN).
BPN

comments: Indicates ductility. Low strain at break (less than 5%) suggests brittle behavior.

basicPropertyValues.val: The elongation of a specimen at the moment of rupture, expressed as a percentage of the original gauge length.
3% strain at break for a glass-filled polyamide.

comments: Critical for injection molding simulations to predict mold filling.

basicPropertyValues.val: A measure of a fluid's resistance to flow.
The melt viscosity of polypropylene at 230°C.

comments: Polymers are non-Newtonian; their shear viscosity decreases as shear rate increases (shear thinning).

basicPropertyValues.val: The viscosity of a fluid when subjected to shear stress; defined as the ratio of shear stress to shear rate.
Viscosity curve data required for Moldflow simulation.

comments: Below Tg, the amorphous phase is frozen; above Tg, polymer chains have mobility.

basicPropertyValues.val: The temperature range where a polymer material transitions from a hard, glassy material to a soft, rubbery material.
Tg of approx. 50°C for dry PA66.
Tg

comments: Crucial for calculating gap and flush in automotive assembly where different materials (steel vs plastic) meet.

basicPropertyValues.val: The fractional change in length per degree of temperature change.
CLTE is distinct in flow-direction vs cross-flow direction for fiber reinforced parts.
CLTE

comments: Used to calculate cooling times in injection molding.

basicPropertyValues.val: The amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a substance per unit of mass.
1700 J/(kg·K) for Polypropylene.
Cp

comments: Often measured via Charpy or Izod notched impact tests.

basicPropertyValues.val: The ability of a material to absorb energy and resist fracture under high speed loading.
High impact strength is required for bumper beams.

comments: Standard metric for stiffness in plastics (ISO 178).

basicPropertyValues.val: The ratio of stress to strain in flexural deformation, or the tendency for a material to resist bending.
Flexural modulus of 8500 MPa for PA66-GF30.

comments: Relevant for snap-fit joints in automotive interiors.

basicPropertyValues.val: The maximum stress a material can withstand in bending before yielding or breaking.
250 MPa for a high-performance composite.

comments: Crucial for PA66 as it absorbs moisture, changing its mechanical properties (conditioning).

basicPropertyValues.val: The concentration of water vapor present in the air or environment surrounding a material.
50% Relative Humidity (standard conditioning atmosphere).

comments: Whether a material is raw material depends on its participation in a particular production or recycling workflow.

basicPropertyValues.val: A material entity that is intended to be consumed, transformed, or incorporated as input in a manufacturing, recycling, or material processing process.
PA66 pellets supplied to an injection molding process as input material.

comments: This class represents the additive material itself; the context-dependent function is represented by additive role.

basicPropertyValues.val: An engineered material intentionally incorporated in a base material in a comparatively small amount to modify processing behavior, performance properties, appearance, or cost.
A UV stabilizer added to an exterior polymer trim material.

comments: The corresponding realizable entity is PMD Core matrix role; this class is used for material entities considered as the continuous phase of a composite.

basicPropertyValues.val: An engineered material that forms the continuous phase of a composite material and binds or embeds reinforcement, filler, or dispersed material constituents.
The PA66 phase in a PA66-GF25 composite.

comments: Values are normally reported for specified test conditions, such as ISO 62 or a specified conditioning atmosphere.

basicPropertyValues.val: A disposition of a material to take up water from a liquid water or humid environment under specified exposure conditions.
The percentage mass increase of a polyamide specimen after immersion in water.
An information content entity that specifies material-related characteristics of a product, component, material batch, or material grade.
A data record listing material designation, density, fiber content, and recycled content for an automotive component.
A composition that describes the material constituents and constituent amounts of a product, component, material batch, or material grade.
A composition stating that PA66-GF25 contains a PA66 matrix and nominally 25 percent glass fiber by mass.

comments: This class covers material entities whose origin is a recycling process; the amount or share of recycled content should be represented separately.

basicPropertyValues.val: A material entity that has been recovered from waste, discarded products, by-products, or production residues through a recycling process for subsequent use.
Regranulated polypropylene produced from end-of-life vehicle parts.

comments: Use for the origin state of a material; use recycled material share for quantities or proportions.

basicPropertyValues.val: A quality of a material entity indicating that the material has an origin in a recycling process rather than only in primary material production.
A recycled quality borne by a polymer regrind used in a new injection molding batch.
A realizable entity representing the environmental, carbon, material, or resource footprint associated with a product over a stated scope.
A product footprint report for an alternator covering cradle-to-gate carbon emissions and recycled material content.

comments: The glossary spelling 'recylced material share' was normalized to 'recycled material share'.

basicPropertyValues.val: A relational quality that relates a material, component, or product to the proportion of its material content that originates from recycled sources.
A bumper fascia with 30 percent recycled polypropylene content.
A product footprint that reports greenhouse gas emissions associated with a product over a stated lifecycle scope.
A product carbon footprint in kg CO2 equivalent for one steering system.
A process attribute that quantifies environmental burden associated with executing a process under specified boundary conditions.
Electricity consumption and associated emissions for one injection molding cycle.
A process footprint that quantifies greenhouse gas emissions associated with executing a process.
The carbon footprint of recycling one kilogram of PA66-GF25 scrap.
A process chain that comprises the stages through which a product or material passes from raw material acquisition through production, use, and end-of-life treatment.
The lifecycle of an automotive control unit from material extraction to recycling.
A process that is a distinguishable part of a product or material lifecycle.
Main product production as a lifecycle stage for an automotive component.

comments: The glossary spelling 'raw material acquistion' was normalized to 'raw material acquisition'.

basicPropertyValues.val: A lifecycle stage in which natural resources, secondary materials, or basic feedstocks are obtained and prepared for production.
Obtaining recycled polymer regranulate for later component production.
A lifecycle stage in which raw materials, intermediate products, or components are transformed into the main product.
Injection molding and assembly steps used to produce an automotive housing.
A lifecycle stage in which products, components, or materials are transported, stored, or transferred between supply chain actors.
Shipment of remanufactured components from a supplier to a vehicle manufacturer.
A lifecycle stage in which a product or component has reached the end of its intended use and is directed to reuse, remanufacturing, recycling, recovery, or disposal.
Removal of a component from an end-of-life vehicle for circular value assessment.
A lifecycle stage in which used materials or products are processed to recover material for further use.
Grinding, sorting, and regranulating a polymer component after dismantling.
A product carbon footprint that quantifies a greenhouse gas emission burden as a carbon dioxide equivalent amount for a stated product, process, or scenario.
12.4 kg CO2 equivalent for a selected reuse scenario.
A carbon footprint amount calculated for a recycling scenario.
The kg CO2 equivalent attributed to recycling a dismantled component.
A carbon footprint amount calculated for a remanufacturing scenario.
The kg CO2 equivalent attributed to inspecting, repairing, and remanufacturing a component.
A carbon footprint amount calculated for a reuse scenario.
The kg CO2 equivalent attributed to checking and reusing a component without remanufacturing.

nodes.lbl: carbon footprint amount in new production

basicPropertyValues.val: A carbon footprint amount calculated for a new production scenario.
The kg CO2 equivalent attributed to manufacturing a replacement component from primary material.
A disposition of a material or component to absorb water vapor from a humid environment under specified exposure conditions.
Moisture uptake by a PA66 component during use at 50 percent relative humidity.
A realizable entity representing the usable lifetime expected to remain for a product or component before it no longer satisfies its intended function or circular value criteria.
An alternator estimated to have 24 months of usable service life remaining after inspection.
A realizable entity representing the observed elapsed lifetime of a product or component up to a stated lifecycle event.
The time between first vehicle registration and dismantling of a component.

comments: The glossary spelling 'Non-destructive Distmantlability' was normalized to 'non-destructive dismantlability'.

basicPropertyValues.val: A disposition of a product or component to be dismantled without destroying the product, component, or material portions that are intended for later use.
A clipped interior trim part that can be removed intact for reuse.
A directive information entity that specifies safety-relevant instructions, warnings, or constraints for handling, dismantling, treating, transporting, or reusing a product or component.
A warning to disconnect a high-voltage battery before dismantling a component.
A process in which used materials, components, or products are collected and processed to recover material for further use.
Sorting and regranulating polymer parts from an end-of-life vehicle.
A process in which a product or component is taken apart so that parts, materials, or information can be recovered, assessed, reused, repaired, remanufactured, recycled, or disposed.
Removing a vehicle door module from an end-of-life vehicle before quality assessment.
A plan specification that provides instructions for dismantling a product or component so that materials can be routed to appropriate recycling treatment.
A guideline specifying how to separate a polymer housing from attached metal inserts before recycling.
A disposition of a product or material to be recycled while preserving a specified level of material quality.
A polymer component designed so that the recovered polymer remains suitable for automotive applications.
A disposition of a product, component, or material to be practically available for recycling in a technical cycle.
A component whose material can be separated and accepted by an established recycling route.
A plan specification that describes the actions, order, tools, and constraints for safely and correctly dismantling a product or component.
Stepwise instructions for removing a battery module from a vehicle.
A disposition of a product or component to be dismantled under stated technical, safety, time, and damage constraints.
A bolted assembly has higher dismantlability than an inseparably bonded assembly when reuse is intended.
A realizable entity representing the estimated average time required to dismantle a product or component under specified conditions.
An average dismantling time of 12 minutes for removal of a door module.
A process used to treat a dismantled component, product, or material after removal from its original assembly.
Mechanical shredding, cleaning, or material separation applied after dismantling.
An information content entity that recommends preferred treatment techniques for a dismantled product, component, or material in a given circular economy scenario.
A recommendation to remove electronics before shredding a polymer-rich component.
Software used to support, guide, document, or validate the disassembly or dismantling of a product or component.
A workshop application that displays product-specific disassembly instructions.
A device used to carry out a disassembly or dismantling operation on a product or component.
A puller tool used to remove a clipped trim component without damage.
A plan specification that describes how parts are joined, fixed, or released in an assembly.
A specification that a housing is fastened with Torx screws and snap-fit clips.
A plan specification that orders the steps required to dismantle a product or component.
Remove cover, disconnect cable, release clips, and lift out module.
A document or figure that depicts a product or assembly with parts spatially separated to show their relative positions and dismantling relationships.
An exploded-view diagram showing screws, clips, housing shells, and subcomponents of a control unit.
An identifier that records the name of a material sub-group in a material classification scheme.
The VDA 231-106 sub-group name 'cast aluminium alloys'.
An identifier that records the code or number of a material sub-group in a material classification scheme.
The VDA 231-106 sub-group code '2.1.1'.
An identifier that records the name of a material main group in a material classification scheme.
The VDA 231-106 main group name 'polymer materials'.
An identifier that records the code or number of a material main group in a material classification scheme.
The VDA 231-106 main group code '5'.
A process in which a discarded or removed product or component is used again while retaining its original function.
A dismantled and inspected body control module installed in another vehicle.
A disposition of a product or component to satisfy requirements for an intended circular economy route, based on relevant condition, completeness, functional, and standard-based criteria.
A reused body part assigned a quality class after inspection for dents, corrosion, paint damage, and completeness.
A disposition of a product or component to require specific test protocols or uncommon equipment before it can be accepted for reuse, repair, remanufacturing, or recycling.
A high-voltage component requiring insulation resistance testing before reuse.
A disposition of a product or component to be reused in its original function under stated technical, legal, safety, and quality constraints.
A complete undamaged headlamp assembly that can be installed as a replacement part.
A quality of a body part component that characterizes its externally relevant condition, such as paint damage, dents, corrosion, and component-specific defects.
A door panel quality assessment based on dents and paint scratches.
A quality that records the grade assigned to a product or component according to a stated quality standard or assessment scheme.
A, B, or C quality grade assigned to a used car part.
A quality of a product or component indicating whether required subcomponents, fasteners, interfaces, or accessories are present.
A mirror assembly assessed as complete because housing, glass, wiring, and mounting clips are present.
A plan specification that defines the quality assessment criteria applied to a reused, repaired, remanufactured, or recycled product or component.
VDI 4080 used as the assessment standard for reuse quality.
A quality of a product or component indicating whether its mounting points, clips, tabs, or attachment features are intact and functional.
A bumper bracket with all mounting tabs undamaged.
A quality of a product, component, or material surface indicating the degree to which it is free from oil, dust, residues, and other contaminants.
A cleaned polymer surface acceptable for adhesive bonding during reuse preparation.
A temporal quality that estimates or records the time required to repair a specified damage or defect on a product or component.
The time needed to repair a paint scratch and small dent on a door panel.
A quality that records the number of cracks observed on a product, component, coating, or material surface.
Two cracks recorded in the paint layer during dismantling inspection.
A quality of a sheet metal or body component indicating the presence or extent of dents or deformations that impair the component shape.
A quarter panel with two visible dents after vehicle use.
A quality that quantifies the surface area of visible damage on a product, component, coating, or material surface.
A paint damage area of 50 square centimeters on a door panel.
A quality of a painted product or component indicating damage to the paintwork, such as scratches, chips, cracks, or coating loss.
Stone-chip damage on a painted bumper cover.
An identifier assigned to a vehicle according to a vehicle identification number scheme and used to identify the individual vehicle.
A 17-character VIN read from a vehicle during dismantling intake.
An identifier that records the name by which a manufacturer is referred to in a data exchange, contract, product record, or manufacturer system.
The manufacturer name 'Schaeffler' in a supplier record.
A site at which a manufacturer carries out production, assembly, treatment, storage, or related industrial operations.
A specific plant where a material batch or component was produced.

comments: This is modeled as a Catena-X-specific subtype of the existing business partner number class.

basicPropertyValues.val: A business partner number used in Catena-X to identify a legal entity, site, or address participating in automotive data exchange.
A Catena-X BPNL identifier for a supplier legal entity.
BPN
An identifier assigned to an individual vehicle according to a vehicle identification number scheme.
A 17-character vehicle identification number used in a dismantling record.
An identifier that records the model designation of a vehicle type as defined by a manufacturer.
A manufacturer-specific vehicle model name used in a product data record.
An information content entity that aggregates identifiers, specifications, usage data, condition data, or circular economy information about a component.
A component record containing part number, material designation, mileage, and reuse assessment.
A quality that quantifies the travel distance accumulated by a component during use in a vehicle.
A used alternator recorded with 125000 km of component mileage.
A temporal quality that quantifies elapsed time since a component was produced, commissioned, installed, or first used, according to a stated reference event.
A component age of six years measured from vehicle production date to dismantling date.
A date value specification that records the calendar year in which a product, component, vehicle, or facility was constructed or manufactured.
The year 2019 recorded as the construction year of a vehicle.
A quality of an environment that is relevant to material behavior, processing, testing, storage, use, or degradation.
A conditioning atmosphere with specified temperature and relative humidity.

comments: The role borne by such a material in a composite is PMD Core filler role.

basicPropertyValues.val: An engineered material incorporated in another material to occupy volume, modify properties, reduce cost, or contribute reinforcement in a composite or compound.
Glass fibers or mineral particles added to a polymer compound.

comments: The Excel hint to PMD Core filler role was modeled as a material class here because the glossary term names the reinforcing material, not only the role.

basicPropertyValues.val: A filler material that is incorporated into a matrix material to improve load-bearing, stiffness, strength, impact, or dimensional performance.
Chopped glass fiber used as reinforcement in PA66-GF25.
A composition data item that records additional material constituents or content fractions not captured by the primary material composition fields.
A data item recording minor additive content in a polymer compound.

comments: The Excel source references ISO 15512 for plastics water content measurement.

basicPropertyValues.val: A mass proportion that quantifies the amount of water contained in a material under specified measurement or conditioning conditions.
Water content of a polyamide granulate measured before injection molding.

comments: The Excel source references ISO 3451 for ash content, often used to estimate inorganic filler content.

basicPropertyValues.val: A mass proportion that quantifies the inorganic residue remaining after controlled combustion or calcination of a material sample.
Ash content used to estimate glass fiber or mineral filler content in a polymer compound.
An information content entity that assigns a material, component, or product to a material category in a stated classification scheme.
A VDA 231-106 material class assignment for polymer materials.
An identifier that records a short material designation or abbreviation used for a material or material family.
PA66-GF25 as an abbreviated designation for a glass-fiber-reinforced polyamide 66 compound.
A simulation process that represents a manufacturing process and its material, process, geometry, or performance consequences.
A simulation of an injection molding process used to predict filling and cooling behavior.
A manufacturing simulation that represents an injection molding process, including relevant material, mold, filling, packing, cooling, or warpage behavior.
A mold-filling simulation for a PA66-GF25 housing.
A simulation process that represents material or component response under loading conditions where inertial effects are negligible.
A quasistatic finite element simulation of a bracket under service load.
A simulation process that represents heat transfer, temperature distribution, or thermally induced material behavior.
A cooling simulation for an injection molded component.
A strength-related material or simulation output quality that represents stress normal to a reference direction under quasistatic loading.
Normal stress predicted by a quasistatic simulation perpendicular to fiber orientation.
A strength-related material or simulation output quality that represents stress parallel to a reference direction under quasistatic loading.
Stress predicted parallel to fiber orientation in a quasistatic simulation.
A mechanical property or simulation output quality that represents strain normal to a reference direction under quasistatic loading.
Normal strain predicted perpendicular to fiber orientation in a quasistatic simulation.
A mechanical property or simulation output quality that represents strain parallel to a reference direction under quasistatic loading.
Strain predicted parallel to fiber orientation in a quasistatic simulation.
A length-related quality or simulation output that represents elongation or expansion normal to a reference direction.
Thermal expansion normal to fiber orientation in a molded composite.
A length-related quality or simulation output that represents elongation or expansion parallel to a reference direction.
Thermal expansion parallel to fiber orientation in a molded composite.
A length quality that quantifies the characteristic or measured length of fibers in a fiber-containing material.
Mean glass fiber length after injection molding of PA66-GF25.
A mass proportion that quantifies the share of material from rework, regrind, or scrap that is reused within the process or production system that generated it.
The percentage of in-process regrind returned to an injection molding feedstock.
An information content entity that records the source category, provenance, or supply-chain origin of a material.
An origin statement that a polymer fraction is post-consumer automotive recycled material.
A mass proportion that quantifies material content originating from pre-consumer production residues, by-products, rework, or scrap.
The share of molding scrap reused before the product reached a consumer.
A mass proportion that quantifies material content originating from products or materials recovered after consumer or user service.
The share of polypropylene recovered from end-of-life vehicle parts.
A mass proportion that quantifies the share of material from a stated source category, such as primary, secondary, pre-consumer, post-consumer, automotive, or non-automotive sources.
A material source share for secondary automotive recycled content.
A document or information content entity that provides additional evidence, provenance, compliance, or quality information about material origin or recycling.
A supplier declaration documenting recycled material origin and quality.
A mass proportion that quantifies the share of material content originating from automotive sources.
The fraction of recycled polymer sourced from end-of-life vehicle components.
A mass proportion that quantifies the share of material content originating from non-automotive sources.
The fraction of recycled polymer sourced from consumer packaging rather than vehicle components.
A material entity obtained from waste, by-products, production residues, or recovered products and intended to substitute for primary material in a production process.
A recycled polymer regranulate used as feedstock for a new automotive component.
A material entity produced from virgin raw resources and used for the first time in a production process.
Virgin PA66 resin produced from primary chemical feedstocks.
A quality that characterizes a physical extent or dimensional feature of a material entity, product, component, or geometric feature.
Length, width, thickness, or diameter used to characterize an automotive component.
An injection molding process in which plasticized material is injected into a mold cavity, held under pressure, cooled or solidified, and ejected as a shaped part.
Injection molding of a PA66-GF25 connector housing.
An elastic modulus that quantifies the ratio of uniaxial stress to uniaxial strain in the linear elastic region of a material response.
Young's modulus measured from a tensile test of a glass-fiber-reinforced polyamide specimen.
A data item that records noteworthy, unexpected, or abnormal properties observed for a component during use, inspection, dismantling, or circular economy assessment.
A note that a component shows unusual discoloration or deformation compared with its expected state.
A realizable entity representing the observed time required to dismantle a product, vehicle, or component in a specific dismantling event.
The measured time in minutes to remove a component from an end-of-life vehicle.
An information content entity that states the rationale for selecting a reuse, repair, remanufacturing, recycling, recovery, or disposal strategy for a product or component.
A reason stating that reuse was rejected because mounting points were broken.
A directive information entity that specifies the circular economy strategy selected for a product or component.
A selected strategy of reuse, recycling, or remanufacturing for a dismantled component.
A disposition assessment that records how dismantlable a product or component was in an observed dismantling event.
An actual dismantlability assessment from a dismantler after removing a door module.
A carbon footprint amount that records the observed or calculated carbon dioxide equivalent burden of the selected circular economy strategy for a component.
The kg CO2 equivalent resulting from choosing reuse for a dismantled component.
A quality of a vehicle that characterizes its observed state relevant to use, dismantling, reuse, repair, recycling, or valuation.
A vehicle condition assessment recorded at dismantling intake.
A quality of a vehicle that classifies its propulsion architecture or drive configuration.
Battery electric drive, hybrid drive, or combustion engine drive.
A mass quality that quantifies the weight of a vehicle without load under the stated specification conditions.
The empty weight in kilograms from vehicle documentation.
A quality of a vehicle that classifies the vehicle according to its design, construction, or intended purpose.
Passenger car, light commercial vehicle, or heavy truck.
A quality of a material, component, or surface indicating degradation by chemical or electrochemical reaction with its environment.
Rust on a steel mounting bracket observed during dismantling.
An identifier assigned by an original equipment manufacturer to a vehicle model, vehicle project, or model variant.
An OEM project code used to identify a vehicle model in product data.
An identifier that records the vehicle series or platform series associated with a vehicle model or component.
A vehicle series code used in dismantling or parts identification data.
A manufacturer role borne by an organization that designs or produces the original vehicle, component, or system for which circular economy data is recorded.
A vehicle manufacturer acting as original equipment manufacturer for a vehicle series.
A date value specification that records when a vehicle, component, product, or material batch was produced.
The production date of a vehicle read from manufacturer records.
An identifier derived from a vehicle identification number by anonymization or hashing so that the vehicle can be linked across records without exposing the original VIN.
A hashed VIN used in circular economy data exchange.
An identifier or designation by which a manufacturer names a material grade, product line, component, or product in its own records or commercial communication.
A manufacturer trade name such as Ultradur, Zytel, or Grilamid.
Temperature of the input melt in a manufacturing process
Maximum temperature of the input melt in a manufacturing process
Minimum temperature of the input melt in a manufacturing process
Temperature of the mold used in a manufacturing process
Maximum temperature of the mold used in a manufacturing process
Minimum temperature of the mold used in a manufacturing process

comments: Instances of Portions Of Matter whose shape is relevant for their dispostion to participate in a manufacturing process may be (subclasses of) objects that bear a 'blank role' in the context of the manufacturing process.

Material is defined in terms of the three main perspectives that material specifications rely on: the structure of the material ("intensive quality"), the performance of the material ("behavoiral material property") and the processing the material must have undergone ("output of some process").

When defining specific materials/material taxonomies, these three aspects shall be taken into account in the aristotelian ("per genus et differentiam") as differentiation.
The sum of portions of matter of the same type form a portion of matter of that type.

basicPropertyValues.val: Portion of Material
A Material is a Portion Of Matter that may participate in some Manifacturing Process and whose shape is not relevant for its participation in the Manifacuring Process.

comments:
It is also representing a distinct physical or conceptual part of a material or substance.

The open energy ontology OEO defines portion of matter as a subclass of object aggregate, which implies countability. Our portion is not (precisely) countable and thus bearer of intensive properties.

Lome explanation for portion of matter: "What is a molecule cluster at the cellular level? Contrary to the molecular level, at the cellular level of granularity the boundary of each molecule is outside of the level of granular focus. What forms a discontinuous cluster of countable individual molecules and thus bona fide objects at the molecular level, is a continuous and non-countable molecular substance at coarser levels of
granularity. At these coarser levels, the individual molecules cannot be differentiated and demarcated anymore and the cluster as a whole possesses only fiat inner boundaries."

basicPropertyValues.val: A material entity that is not demarcated by any physical discontinuities. At some finer level of granularity it is an object aggregate, at some coarser level of granularity it is a fiat object part,but at this level of granularity it is neither.

comments: We recognize that some of the materials in the subclasses also occur naturally. However, these are not within the scope of our consideration for technical use.

basicPropertyValues.val: An Engineered Material is a Material that is output of a Manufacturing Process.

comments:
Technical materials are complex object aggregates. The properties that are determined for those object aggregates are intrinsically associated with the methodologies employed in the measurement process. This is in contrast to typical 'physical' properties, which like e.g. mass that can be determined to high accuracy independently of the measurement process. 
As a consequence, the behavoiral material properties in the ontology are differeciated according to their measurement method, e.g. Brinell hardness and Vickers hardness are different types of indentation hardness rather than different measures/quantification (GDC) of the materials property 'indentation hardness'.
 The 'unit idenifieres' of such properties (e.g. "HV1", "HV10", "HBW") are strictly speaking not units but rather references to the measurement methodology used to determine a numeric value that quantifies the property.

basicPropertyValues.val:
A property is a material trait in terms of the kind and magnitude of response to a specific imposed stimulus. Generally, definitions of properties are made independent of material shape and size.

(Callister, W.D., Rethwisch, D.G., Materials Science and Engineering, Wiley, 2014)

We only deal with intensive (system-size independent) materrial properties. Extensive properties that depend on the testpiece/specimen/probe size should be found in the respective testing method modules.

comments:
Process attribute for a process has a similar application to specifically dependened continuant (SDC) for an independent continuant (IC). Furthermore, process attribute describes how SDCs of some participants, i.e., ICs, change in a process. 

Specific rates, e.g., tensile rate, cooling rate, etc. should be added as  subclasses of this class.

basicPropertyValues.val: process characteristic
a process attribute is a dependent occurrent that existentially depends on a process.
Tensile rate in a tensile testing process. Cooling rate in a quenching process
A form used to cast an object of a desired shape.
The ASTM grain size is a scalar specifically describing the size of grains in a crystalline material as defined by ASTM standards.
The aggregate state is a morphological quality representing the physical state of a material, such as solid, liquid, or gas.
An application of (an) electric field is the process of subjecting a material to an electric field to observe its behavior or modify its properties.
An application of heat flux describes the process of transferring thermal energy to or from a material, often used in thermal property measurements.
An application of (a) magnetic field is the process of subjecting a material to a magnetic field to study its magnetic properties or induce magnetization.
An application of mechanical load is the process of applying force or stress to a material to observe its response or measure its properties.
The application of (a) substance is the process of applying a material or substance to a surface or within a system.
The bulk is a fiat object part that describes the volume or mass of material considered as a whole, often in contrast to surface or interface phenomena.
A change of temperature is a process describing the variation in thermal energy within a material or system over time or due to external influences.
A Composite Material is an Engineered Material that 'consists of' some Material that has a Matrix role and of some Material that has a Filler role.
A process that involves the systematic use of computational methods and tools to perform simulations, analyses, or data transformations to achieve specific scientific or engineering goals.

Ein Prozess, der die systematische Verwendung von rechnerischen Methoden und Werkzeugen umfasst, um Simulationen, Analysen oder Datenumwandlungen durchzuführen, um spezifische wissenschaftliche oder technische Ziele zu erreichen.

The crystal structure is a morphologic quality that describes the periodic geometric arrangement of entities in a crystalline material. The finite set of periodic geometric arrangements is described by the 14 possible Bravais Lattices in three-dimensional space.
The defect density is a morphological quality describing the number of defects per unit volume or area in a material, which can affect its mechanical and electronic properties.
The deformation is a process describing a change in the shape, size, or structure of a material under the influence of stress or force.
The density is a universal intensive quality representing the unit mass of a portion of matter per unit volume.
A physical or virtual entity used to perform a specific function or task, often involving measurement, manipulation, or analysis of materials.
Ein physisches oder virtuelles Objekt, das verwendet wird, um eine bestimmte Funktion oder Aufgabe auszuführen, häufig im Zusammenhang mit der Messung, Manipulation oder Analyse von Materialien.
An elastic modulus is a measure of a material's stiffness, defined as the ratio of stress to strain in the elastic deformation region.
The emission radiation is a process describing the release of energy in the form of electromagnetic waves or particles from a material.
An evolution of damage is a process representing the progression of material degradation under mechanical, thermal, or chemical stresses.

comments: It is often composed of silica-based compounds.

basicPropertyValues.val: A glass is an engineered material present as an amorphous solid typically formed by rapid cooling of a melt.
Korn
grain
A crystal grain is a crystal that is part of a polycrystal.
The hardness is a mechanical property used as a measure of a material's resistance to localized plastic deformation, often tested by indentation or scratch methods.
The heat capacity is a thermal property describing the amount of heat energy required to raise the temperature of a material by a given amount.
An Injection Molding Machine is a Device used to produce plastic parts by injecting molten plastic into molds under high pressure.
Eine Spritzgießmaschine ist ein Gerät, das verwendet wird, um Kunststoffteile durch Einspritzen von geschmolzenem Kunststoff in Formen unter hohem Druck herzustellen.
An irradiation process is a process in which a radiation source (bearing a stimulus role) emits radiation towards a material entity (bearing the target role)

comments:
Manufacturing processes alternate qualities of materials, i.e., a value specification of some quality of a material entity, which is a specified input to the process, cannot be the same as a value specification of the same quality of another material entity, which is a specified output of the process. 

Unfortunately is is not possible to write down such axiom based on OWL. Semi-working solution are the temporally qualified continuants + SPARQL constrains.

basicPropertyValues.val:
A planned process that is driven by the primary intent to transform objects
A manufacturing process is always a transformative process.
Ein geplannter Prozess, der von der primären Absicht angetrieben wird, Objekte zu transformieren. Ein Herstellungsprozess ist immer ein Transformationsprozess.
Matrix is the role of a PortionOfConnectedMatter that implies to host the Filler.

A mechanical property is a material property that inheres in a material M when an object O_target that consists of M is stimulated in a process through an interaction with an object O_stimulus and M changes its stress/strain/..(mechanical?) SDCs.

TODO: Check general class axiom and defintion altogether !!!
The crystallographic texture is a morphological quality describing the arrangement and orientation of grains in a metallic material, observed through crystallographic analysis.
Natural organic materials are materials derived from natural biological sources, primarily composed of carbon-based compounds.
The occurrence of (a) chemical reaction is the process in which substances interact to form new chemical compounds or alter their molecular structure.
The occurrence of (an) electric field is a process describing the presence or generation of an electric field in a material or system.
The occurrence of (a) magenetic field is a process describing the presence or generation of a magnetic field in a material or system.
An optical property is a material property representing the characteristics that describe how a material interacts with light, including reflection, refraction, and absorption.
Passing of time is a process that describes the progression of time, which can influence material aging and property changes.
A phase boundary is a realizable entity that is realized by the transition between distinct phases.

A polymer is an engineered material that consists of many repeated subunits (monomers) to form long chains. The atoms within a polymer chain are bonded together by covalent bonds. The intermolecular forces between polymer chains are typically represented by Van der Waals forces and hydrogen bonds.
A material entity aggregate that is a mereological sum of separate material entities, which adhere to one another through chemical bonds or physical junctions that go beyond gravity.
the atoms of a molecule, the molecules forming the membrane of a cell, the epidermis in a human body

A material entity aggregate that is a mereological sum of scattered (i.e. spatially separated) material entities, which do not adhere to one another through chemical bonds or physical junctions but, instead, relate to one another merely on grounds of metric proximity. The material entities are separated from one another through space or through other material entities that do not belong to the group.
a heap of stones, a colony of honeybees, a group of synapses, the trees of a forest, the canopy of a forest, the fish of a shoal, a group of commuters on the subway, the patients in a hospital

comments: The pressure is commonly measured in Pascals.

basicPropertyValues.val: The pressure is a thermodynamic quality describing the force exerted per unit area on a material.
Primary Forming
A manufacturing process that creates a defined workpiece from shapeless material by producing cohesion, whereby the characteristic properties of the material become visible in the end product.

Ein Herstellungsprozess, der aus formlosem Stoff durch das Herstellen von Kohäsion ein definiertes Werkstück schafft, wobei die charakteristischen Eigenschaften des Materials im Endprodukt sichtbar werden.
Casting, Sintering

nodes.lbl: primary shaping from the plastic state

basicPropertyValues.val: A Primary Shaping process that involves forming materials from a plastic state.
Ein Urformprozess, der das Formen von Materialien aus einem plastischen Zustand beinhaltet.
Thermoforming of plastic sheets.
A Computing Process that models the behavior of a system over time using mathematical or computational techniques.
Ein Datenverarbeitungsprozess, der das Verhalten eines Systems im Laufe der Zeit mit mathematischen oder rechnerischen Techniken modelliert
The specific surface area is a morphologic quality describing the total surface area of a material per unit of mass or volume.
The stiffness is a mechanical property describing the resistance of a material to deformation under an applied force.
The stimulating process is a process describing the application of an external influence, such as force, heat, or radiation, to study material response.
The strength is a mechanical property describing the maximum stress a material can withstand before failure.

comments: The concept refers to an absolute tempeature, not to be mistaken with temperature difference.

To express that an object has a temperature, the property 'intensive bearer of' can be used. It implies a chain of: object consists of some portion of matter and portion of matter bearer of some temperature.

basicPropertyValues.val: The temperature is a fundamental intensive quality representing the average atomic or molcular movement or vibration of a portion of matter.
A thermal property is a material property representing attributes of a material that describe its behavior under temperature changes, such as thermal conductivity and expansion.
A waver irradiation is an irradiation describing an exposure of a material or system to electromagnetic waves or radiation.
Filler is the role of a PortionOfDisconnectedMatter that implies being hosted in a Matrix.
A crystal is an object that has part some entities that concretize a Bravais lattice.
The porosity is a quality describing the fraction of the materials (enclosing) spatial region occupied by pores.

The metallic grain structures is a categorical value specification that specifies value of the  metallic grain structure quality. It describes the morphology of polycrystalline metallic materials. Polycrystalline metallic materials will typically form their grain structures as one of those categories or a transition or mixture state of those.

comments:
TODO: possibly extend for 2D (Graphene etc)

More elaborate crystal descriptions may be found in http://emmo.info/domain-crystallography/crystallography or https://purls.helmholtz-metadaten.de/disos/cso

basicPropertyValues.val: The Bravais lattice is a categorical value specification that specifies value of the crystal structure quality. It describes the possible geometric arrangement of lattice points in a crystal. The present descriptor is limited to the 14 possible three-dimensional arrangements.
concentration
fraction
A Proportion is a relational quality between two entites (the whole and the part) which quantifies the relation between the whole and its part.
The Mass Proportion is a Proportion which quantifies the mass of the part relative to the mass of the whole.
Molar Ratio
The Molar Proportion is the proportion which quantifies the entities count of the part in relation to the entites count of the whole.

The molar ratio of hydrogen gas H2 molecules to water H2O molecules is 1/1 in the oxyhydrogen reaction. The molar ratio of oxigen molecules O2 to water molecules is 2/1.
The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to average atmosphere air is 420 ppm as of 2022 AD.
The Mass Proportion is a Proportion which quantifies the volume of the part relative to the volume of the whole.
Polycrystalline structure
Polycrystal is a connected object aggregate that has continuant part some crystal grains
An intensive quality describing the lower length scale object aggregate (grains) that is part of the material.
The aggregate state value is a categorical value specification that specifies value of the aggregatge state quality.
Point property
An intensive quality is a qualty that inheres in only portion of matter and thus is independent of the bearers (system-) size.
Size is the quality of a material entity that describes its spatial extend.
A process chain is a process that 'has contiuant part' subprocesses S1...Sn where S1...Sn precede each other and where some object or object aggregate that are output of Sn are then input to Sn+1.
An extensive quality is a qualty that inheres in only object or object aggregate or fiat object part or chemical entity and is dependent on the bearers (system-) size.
Composition is a morphological quality describing the types and proportions of compounds (but not chemical elements) present in a portion of matter.
4 vol.% nitric acid solution, Styrene-Butadiene-Styrene (SBS) Block Copolymer with 50 vol.% of both styrene and butadiene
metallic grain structure characterizes the distinct phases in the microscopic structure of a metallic material. It concretizes ferrite, austenite, martensite, etc.

comments: Length is a size that describes the spacial extend of its bearer in one dimension.

basicPropertyValues.val: dimension
Length is a one dimensional size.
A Thermoplastic is a Polymer that becomes moldable when heated and solidifies upon cooling.
Official definition can be found in: DIN 8580, group 1.2.2 “Spritzgießen”.
Offizielle Definition findet man in: DIN 8580, Gruppe 1.2.2 „Spritzgießen“.
injection moulding; plastic injection molding

A primary shaping process in which plastic granules are plasticised in an injection unit and the melt is injected under high pressure into a closed mold cavity, where it cools and solidifies to the final part shape.

Urformverfahren, bei dem Kunststoffgranulat in einem Plastifizieraggregat aufgeschmolzen und die Schmelze mit hohem Druck in einen geschlossenen Formhohlraum eingespritzt wird, wo sie verdichtet, abkühlt und zum Formteil erstarrt.
Injection molding of polypropylene housings.

definition.val: a core relation that holds between a part and its whole
a core relation that holds between a whole and its part

basicPropertyValues.val: b has realization c =Def c realizes b
As for realizes

(Elucidation) realizes is a relation between a process b and realizable entity c such that c inheres in some d & for all t, if b has participant d then c exists & the type instantiated by b is correlated with the type instantiated by c
A balding process realizes a disposition to go bald; a studying process realizes a student role; a process of pumping blood realizes the pumping function of a heart

definition.val: A (currently) primitive relation that relates an information artifact to an entity.

A primitive, instance-level, relation obtaining between an information content entity and some portion of reality. Denotation is what happens when someone creates an information content entity E in order to specifically refer to something. The only relation between E and the thing is that E can be used to 'pick out' the thing. This relation connects those two together. Freedictionary.com sense 3: To signify directly; refer to specifically
inverse of the relation 'denotes'
The inverse property of is specified input of

A relation between a planned process and a continuant participating in that process that is not created during  the process. The presence of the continuant during the process is explicitly specified in the plan specification which the process realizes the concretization of.
The inverse property of is specified output of

A relation between a planned process and a continuant participating in that process. The presence of the continuant at the end of the process is explicitly specified in the objective specification which the process realizes the concretization of.
This relation obtains between a planned process and a objective specification when the criteria specified in the objective specification are met at the end of the planned process.
A relation between a value specification and an entity which the specification is about.
a relation between a specifically dependent continuant (the characteristic) and any other entity (the bearer), in which the characteristic depends on the bearer for its existence.
Inverse of characteristic_of
a relation between a continuant and a process, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process
a relation between a process and a continuant, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

A relationship between a generically dependent continuant and a specifically dependent continuant or process, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant or process in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant or process also depends on that same independent continuant. A generically dependent continuant may be concretized as multiple specifically dependent continuants or processes.

A relationship between a specifically dependent continuant or process and a generically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant or process also depends on that same independent continuant. Multiple specifically dependent continuants or processes can concretize the same generically dependent continuant.
a relation between a function and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between a quality and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between a role and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a function, in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a quality, in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a role, in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a disposition, in which the disposition specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
inverse of has disposition
p has output c iff c is a participant in p, c is present at the end of p, and c is not present in the same state at the beginning of p.
inverse of has output
A relation between an assay and a characteristic, in which the assay generates a data item which is a measure of a characteristic.
Inverse of 'assay measures characteristic'
relationship between a planned process and the plan specification that it carries out; it is defined as equivalent to the composed relationship (realizes o concretizes)

basicPropertyValues.val: Inverse of 'is about'.
A relation between an entity and a value specification which is about this entity.
inverse of responds with
The realizable entity must be "stimulated" by some Stimulus in order to respond with the Response. The bearer of the realizable entity must participate in Stimulation as well as in Response.

definition.val: Class has all its metadata, but is either not guaranteed to be in its final location in the asserted IS_A hierarchy or refers to another class that is not complete.
Term created to ease viewing/sort terms for development purpose, and will not be included in a release

Class has undergone final review, is ready for use, and will be included in the next release. Any class lacking "ready_for_release" should be considered likely to change place in hierarchy, have its definition refined, or be obsoleted in the next release.  Those classes deemed "ready_for_release" will also derived from a chain of ancestor classes that are also "ready_for_release."
Class is being worked on; however, the metadata (including definition) are not complete or sufficiently clear to the branch editors.
Nothing done yet beyond assigning a unique class ID and proposing a preferred term.
All definitions, placement in the asserted IS_A hierarchy and required minimal metadata are complete. The class is awaiting a final review by someone other than the term editor.

nodes.lbl: to be replaced with external ontology term

definition.val: Terms with this status should eventually replaced with a term from another ontology.
A term that is metadata complete, has been reviewed, and problems have been identified that require discussion before release. Such a term requires editor note(s) to identify the outstanding issues.

A state where the bonds between entites trasmit shear forces.
A state where the bonds of the entites transmit no shear force.
A state where the entities have no bonding.
An aggregate state where the entites are atom nuclei and have no bonds.
A gaseous state where the gas entities are atoms.
A state with strong bindings between entites that do not transmit shear force.
A state where some bonds transmit shear stresses and some do not.
A state with frictionless binding that transmits no shear force between entites.
A state that exhibits suprafluid and solid properties.

definition.val: The official definition, explaining the meaning of a class or property. Shall be Aristotelian, formalized and normalized. Can be augmented with colloquial definitions.

basicPropertyValues.val: A textual annotation used to specify a commonly accepted abbreviation, acronym, or shortened form of a class label. This property is intended to support concise referencing of ontology classes, especially when standard abbreviations are widely used in practice.
"DNA" for "Deoxyribonucleic Acid"
